Subtitle: Reconsidering AI in mid-2026
Press enter or click to view image in full size

The Thinker / ChatGPT 5 Digital Art / Prompt: Russ MurrayHuman Introduction
Each summer, as June melts into July — more and more literally with each passing year — my average daily workload falls as the average daily temperature rises. Of course, it’s not (only) the heat that slows everything down when July arrives, but also the inexorable, annual “Vacation-Anticipation-Seasonal-Ecstasy” (VASE) kicking in!
Today, Friday July 3, 2026, is the official holiday for my country’s 250th “birthday” tomorrow. To celebrate, I did a little catch-up work in the morning, cleared my inbox, weeded and watered the garden as the “shade” temperature climbed towards 100°F (~38°C), then ran inside to recover. After the air conditioning resurrected me, I edited and published a blog post for work, after which I ended up distraction-and-doom-scrolling social media. When I tired of that, my mind turned to reflection, as I looked out at heat shimmer above street pavement and neighboring rooftops.
Often these days, when watching the world burn and/or reflecting on life in general, my thoughts turn to AI. It’s not that I’m AI-obsessed per se, but rather that it’s everywhere, on everyone’s lips, in-your-face, unavoidable, and all over the news (just like crypto a few years ago). And, unfortunately, as a…
